rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the implementation scope when bindings depend on subcomponents or internal connections.
Ensure all referenced ports and data types in the annex are visible at the target scope (type or implementation).
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by only referencing features or subcomponents explicitly declared in the model scope.
Use implementation-level annexes for bindings that require access to subcomponent ports or internal state.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use `annex agree {** ... **};` with `guarantee`, `eq`, or `assign` clauses valid in their scope.
Logical expressions must use AGREE types: int, real, bool — never AADL property names as variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or internal paths in component type annexes.
Avoid introducing AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
